title: Compensation assembly for fluid injection line of intravascular catheter system
abstract: A fluid injection line compensation assembly for an intravascular catheter system including a balloon catheter having a guidewire lumen and a cryoballoon, includes a fluid injection line, a proximal fluid injection line stop, and a distal fluid injection line stop. The fluid injection line has a fluid discharge region positioned within the cryoballoon. The fluid discharge region includes a fluid port so that cryogenic fluid can be distributed from the fluid injection line to within the cryoballoon. The fluid discharge region is not affixed to the guidewire lumen. The proximal fluid injection line stop is positioned adjacent to the guidewire lumen, the proximal fluid injection line stop being positioned within the cryoballoon. The distal fluid injection line stop is positioned adjacent to the guidewire lumen so that the fluid discharge region is positioned between the proximal fluid injection line stop and the distal fluid injection line stop.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A cryoablation catheter comprising: a catheter shaft; a guidewire lumen disposed within and slidable relative to the shaft; a cryoballoon having a first end portion secured to a distal end of the shaft, and a second end portion attached to the guidewire lumen distal to the distal end of the shaft; and a fluid injection line having a fluid discharge region positioned within the cryoballoon, the fluid discharge region including a fluid port so that cryogenic fluid can be distributed from the fluid injection line to within the cryoballoon, the fluid discharge region being movable relative to the guidewire lumen. 2. The cryoablation catheter of claim 1 , further comprising: a proximal fluid injection line stop that is positioned adjacent to the guidewire lumen, the proximal fluid injection line stop being positioned within the cryoballoon; and a distal fluid injection line stop that is positioned adjacent to the guidewire lumen so that the fluid discharge region is positioned between the proximal fluid injection line stop and the distal fluid injection line stop. 3. The cryoablation catheter of either of claims 1 or 2, wherein at least one of the proximal fluid injection line stop and the distal fluid injection line stop encircles the guidewire lumen. 4. The cryoablation catheter of any of claims 1 -3, further including a frictional element that frictionally maintains the positioning of the fluid injection line absent movement of the guidewire lumen. 5. The cryoablation catheter of any of claims 1 -4, further comprising a handle assembly coupled to a proximal end of the shaft and including a handle manifold through which the guidewire lumen and the fluid injection line slidably extend, and further including a guidewire lumen sealer that forms a seal around the guidewire lumen. 6. The cryoablation catheter of claim 5, wherein the guidewire lumen sealer does not bias the guidewire lumen in either a proximal or a distal direction relative to the cryoballoon. 7. The cryoablation catheter of either of claims 5 or 6, wherein the guidewire lumen sealer includes one or more O-rings disposed within the handle manifold and around the guidewire lumen. 8. The cryoablation catheter of any of claims 5-7, wherein the guidewire lumen sealer can be tuned to vary an amount of friction between the guidewire lumen sealer and the fluid injection line. 9. The cryoablation catheter of any of claims 1 -8, further comprising: a fluid injection line stop that is positioned within the cryoballoon; a distal bias stop that is secured to the fluid injection line; and a fluid injection line bias that imparts a bias force upon the distal bias stop to bias the fluid injection line in a direction toward the fluid injection line stop. 10. The cryoablation catheter of claim 9, wherein the distal region is biased by the fluid injection line bias to remain in substantially the same position relative to the guidewire lumen whether the cryoballoon is in an inflated state or a deflated state. 1 1 . The cryoablation catheter of either of claims 9 or 10, wherein the distal bias stop and the fluid injection line bias are disposed within the handle manifold. 12. The cryoablation catheter of any of claims 9-11 , further comprising a proximal bias stop disposed within the handle manifold, wherein the fluid injection line bias is positioned against the proximal bias stop. 13. The cryoablation catheter of claim any of claims 9-12, wherein the fluid injection line further comprises a proximal region disposed within the handle manifold, the proximal region of the fluid injection line being configured to form a compression spring that defines the fluid injection line bias. 14. The cryoablation catheter of any of claims 1 -13, wherein the fluid discharge region is movable in a longitudinal direction along the guidewire lumen. 15. The cryoablation catheter of any of claims 1 -14, further comprising a coupler that holds a portion of the fluid injection line adjacent to a portion of the guidewire lumen.
